insight:
Yesterday was a solid recovery day with your body showing excellent signs of preparation for Chester. With only 5,650 steps and no formal training, you gave your legs the rest they need just 5 days out from race day. Your 2,389 calories burned and 7.2 hours of sleep show your metabolism is still active while allowing proper recovery time.

Your HRV at 108ms sits perfectly in your optimal baseline range of 93-116ms, and that low stress level of 22/100 confirms your body is handling the taper well. With your resting heart rate at a strong 47 bpm, these metrics are screaming "go as planned" for today's session - no need to modify intensity or volume.

Focus on hitting your 2,300 calorie target today since you were close yesterday at 2,389 burned. With race week here, prioritize easily digestible carbs and don't skip meals. Your body's primed and ready - trust the process and fuel it properly for Sunday's big day.
